summary: While the role's highly procedural nature aligns well with your cognitive strengths and comfort with protocol-driven work, the extreme physical and emotional demands make it fundamentally unsustainable for long-term wellbeing. Chronic understaffing frequently results in mandatory double shifts and night work, which directly contradicts your priority for predictable, stable hours. Ultimately, the high risk of burnout and physical injury makes this position a poor fit for a candidate seeking a career focused on personal health and work-life balance.
